messages.content: is javascript case sensetive?

Yes, JavaScript is case sensitive which means that it differentiates between uppercase and lowercase letters in variable names, function names, and keywords. For example, variable "myVariable" and "myvariable" are different in JavaScript.
a user can never delete or disable cookies?

As an AI language model, I cannot assert the absolute precision of any statement, but I can explain that a user can delete or disable cookies if they choose to do so. Users can delete cookies manually from their web browser settings, or they can use specialized software tools that clear cookies automatically. Additionally, most web browsers offer the option to disable cookies. However, disabling cookies may impact the functionality of some websites and prevent users from accessing certain features or services.
a user can never delete or disable cookies? yes or no
No, a user can delete or disable cookies.
java script math.round(100.5)?

The `Math.round()` method rounds a number to the nearest integer. If the decimal part of the number is less than 0.5, it rounds down, otherwise, it rounds up. 

So, `Math.round(100.5)` will return `101` because 0.5 rounds up to the nearest integer which is 101.
for in javascript
how to call function in JS

In JavaScript, a function can be called by using its name and parentheses. You can also pass arguments to the function inside the parentheses.

Here's an example of calling a function named `myFunction` without any arguments:

```
myFunction();
```

And here's an example of calling the same function with an argument:

```
myFunction("Hello, world!");
```

When you call a function, the JavaScript interpreter searches for a function with that name and executes the code inside it. If the function takes any parameters, you can pass them as comma-separated values inside the parentheses.

You can also assign the result of a function call to a variable like this:

```
let result = myFunction();
```

This assigns the return value of `myFunction` to the `result` variable. Note that not all functions have return values, in that case, the variable will be assigned a value of `undefined`.
do you use "call" to call function in JS?
HTML HYPERLINK

In HTML, a hyperlink is a clickable link that takes the user to a specified URL or navigates to a different section on the current page. Here's an example of creating a hyperlink in HTML: ```html Click here to visit Example website ``` In this example, the `` element is used to create a hyperlink. The `href` attribute specifies the URL that the hyperlink will link to. The text "Click here to visit Example website" is the text that the user clicks on to follow the link. To create a hyperlink that navigates to a different section on the same page, you can use an anchor link like this: ```html Go to Section 1 ``` In this example, the `href` attribute specifies the ID of the section on the page that you want to navigate to. The ID is prefixed with a `#` symbol. This will navigate the user to the section on the same page when clicked. The `name` attribute can also be used in place of `id` in older versions of HTML. It is important to note that hyperlinks can also be used with JavaScript to add interactivity to a webpage.

html insert image

This can be a local file or a URL to an image on the internet. - `alt` attribute specifies the description of the image. This is important for accessibility. If the image fails to load, the `alt` attribute informs the user what the image should look like. Here's an example of how to add an image to an HTML file: ```html

Welcome to my webpage!

    1. ` (list item) element. Here is an example of how to create an unordered list: ```html

        3. Third item

          ` element defines the ordered list and each item is represented by an `
        1. ` element. You can also nest lists within each other, as shown in the following example: ```html
          1. First item
            • Nested item 1
            • Nested item 2
          2. Second item
          ``` In this example, the first list item of the ordered list contains an unordered list nested inside it. Lists are useful for presenting information in an organized and easy-to-read format.
html add comment

In HTML, you can add comments to your code by using the `` syntax. Here's an example: ```html My Website

Welcome to my website!

Some text here...

``` In this example, a comment has been added between the `` tags. The comment does not affect the display of the webpage and is ignored by the browser. You can use comments in your HTML code to provide additional information to other developers who might be working on the project with you. Comments can also be a useful tool when you are debugging your code, as you can comment out sections of code to see how your webpage behaves without them.

how to attach css to html

In HTML, you can attach CSS styles to your HTML document in several ways, but the most common method is by using the `` element to link to an external CSS file. Here is an example of how to link an external CSS file to an HTML document: 1. Create a new CSS file with a `.css` extension. For example, `style.css`. 2. Add your CSS styles to the file. For example: ```css h1 { color: red; font-size: 36px; } p { color: blue; font-size: 18px; } ``` 3. In the HTML document, add a `` element in the `` section to link to the external CSS file. ```html My Website

Welcome to my website!

Some text here...

``` In this example, the `` element is used to link to the `style.css` file using the `href` attribute. The `rel` attribute is set to "stylesheet" to indicate that the linked file is a stylesheet. By linking to an external CSS file, you can keep your HTML and CSS code separate, making it easier to maintain and modify your website's design.
html background colour

In HTML, you can set the background color of your web page by using the CSS `background-color` property. Here is an example of how to set the background color of an HTML document: ```html My Website

Welcome to my website!

Some text here...

``` In this example, the `background-color` property is set to `#f5f5f5`, which is a light gray color. You can also use other values for the `background-color` property, such as color names like "white" or "red", hexadecimal color codes like "#ff0000" (which represents red), or RGB values like "rgb(255, 0, 0)" (which also represents red). Note that the `background-color` property is applied to the entire body of the web page. If you want to set the background color of a specific section or element within the page, you can apply the `background-color` property to that element instead.
html how to open new tab window

In HTML, you can open links in a new browser tab or window by using the `target` attribute with the value of `_blank`. Here is an example of how to open a link in a new window: ```html Visit Example website ``` In this example, the `target` attribute is set to `_blank`, which tells the browser to open the link in a new tab or window. You can also use the `target` attribute to open other types of resources in a new window, such as PDF documents or images. Here is an example: ```html View Example PDF ``` Again, the `target` attribute is set to `_blank`, so the PDF document will open in a new browser tab or window when the user clicks on the link. Note that the exact behavior of the `target` attribute can vary depending on the browser and device being used. Some users may have browser settings that force all links to open in the same window, so it's generally a good idea to avoid overusing this attribute.
html canvas used to

In HTML, the `<canvas>` element is used to create graphics and visual effects on a web page using JavaScript.

The `<canvas>` element provides a rectangular area on a web page on which you can draw graphics and other visual effects using JavaScript code. 

Here is an example of how to create a basic canvas element in HTML:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>My Canvas Example</title>
    <style>
      canvas {
        border: 1px solid black;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <canvas id="myCanvas" width="500" height="500"></canvas>
    <script>
      // JavaScript code goes here
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
```
